human_verification.notes: Reviewed the food safety agriculture specialist package for boundary strictness, authoritative-source grounding, scenario coverage, functionality coverage, escalation posture, and marketplace-ready spec packaging.

review_notes: Readiness evidence is complete across manifest, scenarios, functionality map, research summary, evaluation results, release notes, deployment notes, and marketing notes.
The package explicitly records that runtime remains specification-based until a trusted built version is separately promoted.

Scenario coverage proves incomplete evidence handling, hold-boundary enforcement, repeat incident escalation, seasonal and traceability dependency handling, adjacent specialist handoff, and tenant-specific adaptation.

specialist_owned_evidence: Authority references are grounded in FDA, eCFR, USDA, EPA, APHIS, and Codex sources with explicit refresh and decay policy.
The research summary covers workflow stages, artifacts, systems of record, decision boundaries, domain failure modes, scenario-family coverage, and no blocking unresolved ambiguity.

Scenarios cover normal in-scope work, incomplete evidence, release requested under hold, repeat incident, control-bypass refusal, seasonal field changes, traceability conflicts, adjacent agronomic/veterinary/environmental/grant handoff, missing facts, conflicting policy, and tenant adaptation.
Functionality map declares every shared, workflow-phase, and agriculture industry-profile ability with required inputs, outputs, refusal triggers, and scenario mappings.
Package includes deployment rollback path and explicit separation from retained implementation authority.
notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in local package review.
Ready for market deployment as a specification-based specialty pack with clear escalation into adjacent specialists.
Trusted built promotion remains a separate future decision after repeated usage or explicit runtime approval.
